← all tutorials

Quorum Replication (Dynamo / Cassandra) — interactive QUORUM_RW.md quorum_rw.py

W replicas ack a write, R replicas answer a read. If their circles overlap, the reader sees the fresh value. Drag R and W and watch the intersection — and the consistency verdict — change. All numbers recompute live from the identical R + W > N rule as quorum_rw.py.

① The ring — drag R and W; watch the circles overlap (or not)

What to do: the writer circles the first W replicas clockwise from coordinator R0; the reader circles the first R. Their intersection is gold. The pigeonhole floor R+W−N is the guaranteed minimum overlap; the actual overlap is usually larger.
write set W read set R intersection W∩R not contacted

② The R/W dial — four classic configs (Cassandra CL=ONE/QUORUM/ALL)

What this shows: click a preset to set (W, R) and watch panel ① recompute. The first three have R+W>N (strong); the last has R+W≤N (eventual — disjoint quorums exist).
N = 5

③ Sloppy quorum + hinted handoff — a replica is DOWN

What to do: with N=5, W=3, toggle R1 down. The write must still hit 3 living replicas, so it walks past R1 to R3. R3 keeps a hint ("this really belongs to R1") and forwards it when R1 recovers.
coordinator R0, preference list R0→R1→R2→R3→R4

④ Read repair — stale replica fixed on the fly

What to do: press play. A read of R=3 contacts {R0, R1, R2}; R2 missed the last write (version 1 stale). The reader writes the fresh value back to R2 before returning.
step 0 / 3

⑤ Anti-entropy — Merkle tree comparison (pinned from quorum_rw.py §5)

What this shows: replicas A and B compare Merkle-tree roots. They differ, so they descend into the mismatched subtree and sync only k2. Leaf hashes below are sha1('key|value')[:8], pinned verbatim from quorum_rw.py Section E (the .py is the source of truth for the hashes; the .html demonstrates the comparison algorithm).
check: … JS recompute matches quorum_rw.py GOLD: min_intersection(3,3,5) = 1 and every (W,R) pair with R+W>N intersects.
